title: Add User Badge

story.so_that: I can quickly confirm who I am using the System
story.i_want: see my name and role shown in the header

journey.before: User logs in and navigates to the Search page or Tactic Detail Page without any indication of their identity or role
journey.during: User is actively using the Search page or Tactic Detail Page and needs to confirm their identity and role
journey.after: User can see their name and role displayed in the header (top-right), confirming their identity and maintaining awareness of their permissions

capability_breakdown.core_action: Display user name and role in a badge component in the header
outputs: User badge component visible in header (top-right)
Badge displays user name and role
Badge uses a simple user icon
state_changes: No state changes - this is a read-only display feature

ui: Layout adjustments to accommodate badge in top-right
api: No API changes required - user data already available in application state from authentication/authorization flow

acceptance_criteria.criterion: User sees their name and role in the header when using the Search page
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given I am a User When I use the Search page Then I see my name and role shown in the header
acceptance_criteria.jtbd: Confirm my identity while using the Search page

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Proofreader user sees their name and role in the header when using the Tactic Detail Page
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given I am a User with the Proofreader Role When I am using the Tactic Detail Page Then I can see my name and role shown in the header
acceptance_criteria.jtbd: Confirm my identity and role while reviewing tactics

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Existing Tactic Detail functionality remains unaffected
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given I am a User with the Proofreader Role When I am using the Tactic Detail Page Then existing Tactic Detail functionality works as before
acceptance_criteria.jtbd: Maintain existing functionality while adding new feature

acceptance_criteria.criterion: User badge is only applied to the tactic-builder interface
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given the application has multiple interfaces When the user badge feature is deployed Then it only appears in the tactic-builder interface

acceptance_criteria.criterion: User badge appears in the top-right of the page
acceptance_criteria.gherkin: Given a user is logged in When they view any page in tactic-builder Then the user badge appears in the top-right corner
acceptance_criteria.jtbd: Locate user identity information in expected location

edge_cases.gherkin: Given a user with a name longer than 30 characters When they view the tactic-builder Then the badge displays the name without truncation or overflow

edge_cases.criterion: User with special characters in name
edge_cases.gherkin: Given a user with special characters (accents, unicode) in their name When they view the tactic-builder Then the badge displays the name correctly
edge_cases.jtbd: Display names with special characters correctly

edge_cases.criterion: User with very long role name
edge_cases.gherkin: Given a user with a role name longer than 20 characters When they view the tactic-builder Then the badge displays the role without truncation or overflow

edge_cases.criterion: User session expires while viewing page
edge_cases.gherkin: Given a user is viewing the tactic-builder When their session expires Then the badge should either update or redirect appropriately
Given a user with no assigned role When they view the tactic-builder Then the badge displays their name and shows an appropriate default or empty role indicator
Given a user viewing tactic-builder on a small screen When the page is rendered Then the badge remains visible and usable without obscuring critical content
edge_cases.jtbd: Ensure badge is responsive on small screens

questions.question: What is the exact format for displaying the role name? Should it be the role's display name or system identifier?
Should the badge be clickable/interactive (e.g., to open user profile or logout menu)?
What should happen if user data is not available in the application state?
Should the badge update in real-time if the user's role changes during their session?
Are there any accessibility requirements for the badge (ARIA labels, keyboard navigation)?
What icon library or design system should be used for the user icon?

existing_code: Application state management (Redux/Context/etc) containing user information
new_code: Integration of badge into header layout
infrastructure: Existing authentication system must provide user name and role

blocked_by: Authentication/authorization system must be fully functional and providing user data
blocks: Any features that depend on user identity being visible in the UI
related: Story 35 - Admin Tactic Types (may share header/layout components)

domain_knowledge: User roles in the tactic management system
How roles affect user permissions and workflows
technical_knowledge: CSS/styling approach used in the project
stakeholder_questions: What is the priority of this feature relative to other work?
Are there specific design mockups or style guidelines for the badge?
Should the badge be visible to all users or only certain roles?

recommendation: This is a straightforward UI feature with low complexity and risk. The implementation is well-scoped and depends only on existing authentication/authorization data. Recommended for early sprint inclusion as it provides immediate user experience value with minimal technical risk.
